title: Frozen Permanents of E. coli Cultures

guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"oiceo","text":"This is a simple protocol for preparing frozen permanents of E. coli or other bacterial cells.  The frozen permanents can be stored for years at -80°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Inoculate 5ml LB 100ug/ml ampicillin (or other appropriate antibiotic for your culture) with cells.
Mix 800ul culture with 200ul 100% w/v glycerol (sterile) in sterile screw cap freezer tube. (usually make several tubes)
Bury tube in dry ice until frozen solid.
Overnight to grow culture plus about 15 minutes to complete the rest of the protocol.
Frozen permanents should be good for years if stored at -80°C.
Expression, Detergent Solubilization, and Purification of a Membrane Transporter, the MexB Multidrug Resistance Protein by Forum H. Bhatt and Constance J. Jeffery https://dx.doi.org/10.3791/2134
creator: Constance Jeffery
